brief_summary: Top-down view of the back of a PCB. Four coral-filled horizontal strips applied in an even grid pattern; faint warm-grey rectangles indicate hot-swap socket positions that the strips work around. Right-margin callouts: '3–5 strips · painter's, not Kapton' and 'leave clear around hot-swap sockets'. Caption: 'an even grid across the surface that vibrates most freely'.
data_sources: tape-mod.mdx § 'What the mod is' — 'Apply three to five strips of painter's tape in an even grid across the back of the PCB — the component side, not the switch side.'
tape-mod.mdx § Callout 'Tape and hot-swap socket clearance' — 'Tape strips around the socket area, leaving the sockets clear, is the safer approach on boards with tall hot-swap sockets.'
